title: DANDELION ROOT
description: Dandelion root (Taraxacum officinale) is a traditional medicinal plant from the Asteraceae family, used for centuries in various cultures for its therapeutic properties. It contains bioactive compounds including sesquiterpenoids, caffeoylquinic acids, and flavonoids that contribute to its pharmacological effects. Primarily recognized for its diuretic, anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and hepatoprotective properties, dandelion root is commonly used to support liver health, reduce water retention, and aid digestion.

research.title: Taraxacum—A review on its phytochemical and pharmacological profile
research.summary: Comprehensive review documenting dandelion's traditional use for dyspepsia, liver complaints, and hepatitis, with evidence supporting diuretic, choleretic, anti-inflammatory, anti-oxidative, anti-carcinogenic, analgesic, anti-hyperglycemic, anti-coagulatory and prebiotic effects.

research.title: Common dandelion: a review of its botanical, phytochemical and pharmacological profiles
research.summary: Scientific investigation confirms dandelion's pharmacological potential with demonstrated antioxidant, antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, antiviral, cytotoxic, diuretic and hepatoprotective properties attributed to sesquiterpenoids, caffeoylquinic acids and flavonoids.

research.title: Dandelion (Taraxacum Genus): A Review of Chemical Constituents and Pharmacological Effects
research.summary: Review of dandelion's chemical composition and therapeutic effects, confirming its traditional medicinal uses and identifying key bioactive compounds responsible for its health benefits.

research.title: The phytochemical and pharmacological profile of dandelion
research.summary: Systematic analysis of dandelion's phytochemical constituents and their associated pharmacological activities, supporting its use in traditional medicine systems.

research.title: Dandelion (Taraxacum officinale): A Promising Source of Nutritional and Therapeutic Compounds
research.summary: Evaluation of dandelion as a source of nutritional and therapeutic compounds, highlighting its potential applications in nutraceutical and phytopharmaceutical industries.

sentiment.summary: User feedback indicates limited effectiveness for dandelion root, with one user specifically mentioning trying it for bloating without success. The compound receives minimal positive discussion in biohacking communities compared to other supplements, and appears to be abandoned in favor of more effective alternatives.

effects.label: Limited User Success:
effects.description: One user specifically reported trying dandelion root for bloating without satisfactory results, leading to exploration of other options

effects.label: Traditional vs Modern Use:
effects.description: Strong historical documentation of traditional medicinal use, but limited contemporary user validation in biohacking communities

effects.label: Research-Practice Gap:
effects.description: Scientific literature supports various pharmacological effects, but practical user experiences suggest modest real-world benefits

effects.label: Alternative Preference:
effects.description: Users appear to favor other supplements and interventions over dandelion root for similar health goals

dosage.label: Standard Dosing:
dosage.description: Research reviews discuss various preparations including extracts, teas, and capsules, though specific optimal dosing is not well-established in user communities

dosage.label: Form Variations:
dosage.description: Available as dried root, liquid extracts, capsules, and teas, with traditional preparations varying widely in concentration

dosage.label: Duration Considerations:
dosage.description: Limited user feedback on long-term use protocols or cycling recommendations

dosage.label: Combination Approaches:
dosage.description: Often used as part of broader herbal or supplement protocols rather than as a standalone intervention

sideEffects.label: Diuretic Effects:
sideEffects.description: Increased urination frequency, which may be undesirable for some users and require attention to hydration

sideEffects.label: Digestive Sensitivity:
sideEffects.description: Potential for mild gastrointestinal upset in some individuals, particularly with higher doses

sideEffects.label: Allergic Reactions:
sideEffects.description: Possible allergic responses in individuals sensitive to plants in the Asteraceae family

sideEffects.label: Limited Severe Effects:
sideEffects.description: Generally well-tolerated with minimal serious adverse effects reported in research or user experiences

availability.label: Widely Available:
availability.description: Readily accessible as an over-the-counter supplement in various forms including capsules, teas, and extracts

availability.label: Quality Variability:
availability.description: As with many herbal supplements, product quality and active compound concentrations may vary significantly between manufacturers

availability.label: Legal Status:
availability.description: Fully legal and unregulated as a dietary supplement in most jurisdictions, with no prescription required
